metadata.input_distribution_notes: Run log shows 10+ prior sessions covering ~42 fixes, ~1500 tests, 20 widget types. The prompt has been used for widget-by-widget parity audits, integer-promotion audits, alpha-channel audits, boundary-value differential testing, and composition testing. The contract JSON has 111 features (28 phase-1, 27 phase-2, 50 phase-3, 6 phase-4). Phase-1 features average 11 steps each. Prior sessions operated under earlier prompt versions (no contract JSON). This master-contract prompt is new and has not yet been run. Known failure modes include speed-over-fidelity (skipping methods), test-shaped validation (weak assertions), and reusing existing tests instead of writing new ones. Features include RUST-ONLY (7 features with no C++ counterpart) which have a different step template.

instructions.text: Read the master contract at prompts/master-contract.json at the start of the run.
instructions.notes: First sentence of the prompt. Establishes the contract as the primary data source.

instructions.text: Read CLAUDE.md at the workspace root at the start of the run.
instructions.notes: Second part of first sentence. CLAUDE.md contains code rules, fidelity rules, and Do NOT rules.

instructions.text: Process features in order: read next feature where passes == false, execute its steps, then move to next.
instructions.notes: The main loop. Sequential processing of features.

instructions.text: For each feature, read its steps array and execute each step (read files, compare, report, fix, test).
instructions.notes: Steps are the per-feature instructions from the contract JSON.

instructions.text: After executing a feature's steps, run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings and ensure zero warnings.
instructions.notes: Gate condition 1 of 2. Must pass before marking passes=true.

instructions.text: After executing a feature's steps, run cargo nextest run --workspace and ensure zero failures.
instructions.notes: Gate condition 2 of 2. Must pass before marking passes=true.

instructions.text: After the gate passes, edit the contract JSON to set the feature's passes field to true.
instructions.notes: Modifying the contract is part of the loop.

instructions.text: After setting passes to true, run git add prompts/master-contract.json <changed files> and commit with message 'audit: <feature_id> <summary>'.
instructions.notes: One commit per feature.

instructions.text: Do not enter plan mode.
instructions.notes: Prevents the LLM from using a plan/think tool instead of acting.

instructions.text: Run the bootstrap script at the start to see current progress (done/total features passing, test results).
instructions.notes: Bootstrap gives the agent situational awareness.

instructions.text: Phase 1 features: Compare each C++ method listed in the methods field. Report MATCH/MISMATCH/MISSING for each. Fix MISMATCHes. Write tests.
instructions.notes: Phase 1 workflow summary.

instructions.text: Phase 2 features: For each gap method, determine IMPLEMENTED (different name), NOT_NEEDED (superseded), or NEEDS_IMPLEMENTATION. Implement what's needed, write tests.
instructions.notes: Phase 2 workflow summary.

instructions.text: Phase 3 features: Verify all consolidated C++ methods are present in the Rust file that consolidates multiple C++ classes.
instructions.notes: Phase 3 workflow summary.

instructions.text: Phase 4 features: Review ALL existing tests (1500+) across every test directory for anti-patterns: test-shaped validation, missing side-effect checks, hardcoded-pass inputs, missing modifier setup. Strengthen defective tests. If a strengthened test reveals a real bug, fix it.
instructions.notes: Phase 4 workflow summary. Emphasizes ALL tests.

instructions.text: Phase 1 features have a required_test_layers field. A fix is not complete until ALL required layers have a test. Layers are: unit (#[cfg(test)] module), golden (tests/golden/), pipeline (tests/pipeline/ using PipelineTestHarness).
instructions.notes: Strict requirement, not guidance. The prompt says 'This is not guidance -- it is a requirement'.

instructions.text: When writing tests, follow the patterns in the corresponding test directory (unit tests in source file, pipeline in tests/pipeline/, golden in tests/golden/, integration in tests/integration/).
instructions.notes: Test organization convention.

instructions.text: Phase 1 steps require reading the C++ .cpp file (not just the header) to catch implementation details: static helpers, constructor defaults, and side effects (signals, invalidation, repaint) that headers don't show.
instructions.notes: Explicitly calls out a failure mode: only reading headers misses implementation details.

instructions.text: Features with layer=PIXEL require exact C++ formulas. Integer arithmetic must match. Use (x*257+0x8073)>>16 not f64.
instructions.notes: Fidelity rule for PIXEL layer. Matches CLAUDE.md fidelity rules.

instructions.text: Features with layer=STATE: idiomatic Rust is correct. Only flag behavioral differences. Do NOT flag Rc vs pointers, enum vs int flags.
instructions.notes: Fidelity rule for STATE layer. Prevents false positive MISMATCHes.

instructions.text: Features without a layer field default to STATE fidelity rules.
instructions.notes: Default layer assignment.

instructions.text: Phases execute in order. Before starting a phase, verify its dependency phase is fully complete (every feature has passes: true). Phase 2 depends on phase 1. Phase 3 depends on phase 2. Phase 4 depends on phase 3.
instructions.notes: Phase ordering constraint. If dependency phase has incomplete features, finish those first.

instructions.text: If a later feature requires changing code from a previously-completed feature, you MAY modify that code, but you MUST re-run cargo nextest run --workspace to confirm all previously-passing tests still pass. If any break, fix them before proceeding.
instructions.notes: Cross-feature modification rule. Prevents regressions.

instructions.text: The gate (clippy + tests) does NOT verify you compared every method. If the methods field lists 20 methods and you compared 5, the gate still passes. You must compare ALL listed methods.
instructions.notes: Known failure mode: speed-over-fidelity. The gate is necessary but not sufficient.

instructions.text: Steps are requirements. If a step says 'compare these 20 methods' -- compare all 20, not the ones that seem important.
instructions.notes: Reinforces i-23. Steps are not suggestions.

instructions.text: Tests must assert on specific values and behaviors, not just is_some() or is_ok().
instructions.notes: Known failure mode: test-shaped validation.

instructions.text: If you fix a MISMATCH, write a NEW test. Existing tests didn't catch the bug.
instructions.notes: Known failure mode: reusing existing tests.

instructions.text: Do NOT modify the contract's steps, description, or methods fields.
instructions.notes: Contract immutability rule. Also in contract rules.immutability.

instructions.text: Do NOT modify existing tests (write new tests only).
instructions.notes: Exception: Phase 4 explicitly requires strengthening existing tests. Potential conflict.

instructions.text: Do NOT modify unrelated production code.
instructions.notes: Scope containment.

instructions.text: Do NOT skip a feature. If 0 MISMATCHes found, it still passes -- set passes to true.
instructions.notes: Every feature must be visited regardless of outcome.

instructions.text: Do NOT flag idiomatic Rust as MISMATCH. Read the layer field.
instructions.notes: Prevents false positives on STATE-layer features.

instructions.text: Do NOT treat the gate as proof of completeness. The gate is necessary but not sufficient.
instructions.notes: Metacognitive instruction about the gate's limitations.

instructions.text: Run the completion script at the end to report total progress and list any PENDING features.
instructions.notes: Final status report.

instructions.text: After each feature, append to .workflow/widget-comparison/run-log.md with format: feature_id, description, MATCHes/MISMATCHes/MISSINGs counts, fixes applied, tests added.
instructions.notes: Logging requirement with specific format.

instructions.text: Start with the first feature in phase-1 where passes is false.
instructions.notes: Entry point after bootstrap.

instructions.text: Contract rule: Agent MAY set passes to true. Agent MAY NOT delete features, modify steps, or mark as not applicable.
instructions.notes: From contract rules.immutability. Overlaps with i-27.

instructions.text: Contract rule: passes may only be set to true after clippy clean + all tests pass.
instructions.notes: From contract rules.gate_protocol. Reinforces i-05/i-06.

instructions.text: Contract rule: Commit after each feature. Include the contract JSON.
instructions.notes: From contract rules.commit_protocol. Reinforces i-08.

instructions.text: Contract rule: Check each feature's layer field. PIXEL = exact formulas, GEOMETRY = same algorithm, STATE = idiomatic Rust OK.
instructions.notes: From contract rules.fidelity_layer. Adds GEOMETRY layer not mentioned in prompt .md.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): Read the C++ header file for the feature.
instructions.notes: Step 1 of standard phase-1 template. File path specified per-feature.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): Read the C++ .cpp implementation file for the feature.
instructions.notes: Step 2 of standard phase-1 template. Reinforces i-17.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): Read the Rust source file for the feature.
instructions.notes: Step 3 of standard phase-1 template.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): Compare the N methods listed in the methods field.
instructions.notes: Step 4 of standard phase-1 template. Must compare ALL listed methods.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): Also read the C++ .cpp implementation file -- check for static helpers, default values in constructors, and side effects (signals, invalidation, repaint calls) that the header doesn't show.
instructions.notes: Step 5 of standard phase-1 template. Reinforces i-17.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): For each method, verify constructors set the same default values, and methods produce the same side effects (callback firing, flag invalidation, repaint triggers).
instructions.notes: Step 6 of standard phase-1 template. Specifies what to check.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): For each method, report MATCH, MISMATCH, SUSPECT, or MISSING.
instructions.notes: Step 7 of standard phase-1 template. SUSPECT is an additional category beyond the prompt's MATCH/MISMATCH/MISSING.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): For each MISMATCH, fix the Rust code minimally.
instructions.notes: Step 8 of standard phase-1 template. Emphasizes minimal fixes.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (standard): For each fix, write a test verifying the corrected behavior.
instructions.notes: Step 9 of standard phase-1 template. Reinforces i-26.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (RUST-ONLY variant): No C++ equivalent. Verify internal correctness: no dead code, no panicking paths, edge cases handled.
instructions.notes: Alternate step for 7 RUST-ONLY features with no C++ counterpart.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (RUST-ONLY variant): Write a unit test for any untested edge case found.
instructions.notes: Test writing for RUST-ONLY features.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step (CRITICAL, 2 features): Verify every config field's default value matches C++ emCoreConfig constructor defaults exactly -- these control zoom speed, scroll speed, magnetism radius, thread count, and render quality.
instructions.notes: Special CRITICAL step on ext-model-core_config and ext-widget-core_config_panel features. Elevated priority.

instructions.text: Phase 2 step: Note that some C++ methods are generated by macros (EM_IMPL_OBJ_CLASS, EM_IMPLEMENT_*_REC). If a symbol looks like boilerplate (constructor/destructor/copy), check whether it was macro-generated. Macro boilerplate is typically NOT_NEEDED in Rust.
instructions.notes: Phase-2-specific guidance about C++ macro boilerplate.

instructions.text: Phase 2 step: For each listed C++ method, determine: (a) is it actually needed in the Rust port? (b) if yes, is it already implemented under a different name? (c) if truly missing, is it feasible to implement?
instructions.notes: Phase 2 analysis framework.

instructions.text: Phase 2 step: For each method, mark as IMPLEMENTED (found under different name), NOT_NEEDED (superseded or not applicable), or NEEDS_IMPLEMENTATION (with scope estimate).
instructions.notes: Phase 2 classification categories.

instructions.text: Phase 2 step: For NEEDS_IMPLEMENTATION items, implement them and write a test.
instructions.notes: Phase 2 implementation requirement.

instructions.text: Phase 3 step: Read the Rust source file.
instructions.notes: Phase 3 step 1.

instructions.text: Phase 3 step: Verify the listed C++ symbols are covered in the Rust file.
instructions.notes: Phase 3 step 2. Symbols listed per-feature.

instructions.text: Phase 3 step: Verify all public methods from the consolidated C++ classes have Rust equivalents.
instructions.notes: Phase 3 step 3. Completeness check.

instructions.text: Phase 4 step: List all #[test] functions in the specified test directory.
instructions.notes: Phase 4 step 1. Directory varies per feature (pipeline, golden, behavioral, integration, unit, or src/ inline).

instructions.text: Phase 4 step: For each test function, check 5 anti-patterns: (1) assertions verify SPECIFIC expected values not is_some()/is_ok(), (2) side effects are verified, (3) test would FAIL if implementation were subtly wrong, (4) modifier keys set correctly, (5) test exercises actual behavior not just compilation.
instructions.notes: Phase 4 step 2. The 5-point checklist for each test.

instructions.text: Phase 4 step: Fix any defective test by strengthening its assertions.
instructions.notes: Phase 4 step 3. Note: this conflicts with i-28 (do NOT modify existing tests). Phase 4 is an exception.

instructions.text: Phase 4 step: If a strengthened test now FAILS, that is a real bug. Fix the production code too.
instructions.notes: Phase 4 step 4. Bug-finding through test strengthening.

instructions.text: Phase 4 (inline test variant): grep -rn '#[cfg(test)]' src/ to find all inline test modules.
instructions.notes: review-inline-tests feature uses grep instead of listing a test directory.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Use f64 for logical coordinates, i32 for pixel coordinates, u32 for image dimensions, u8 for color channels.
instructions.notes: Code rule from CLAUDE.md. Applies to any code written during the audit.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Use Color (packed u32 RGBA) for storage. Intermediate blend math in i32 or wider.
instructions.notes: Code rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Use Rc/RefCell for shared state, Weak for parent refs. No Arc/Mutex (single-threaded UI tree).
instructions.notes: Ownership rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Use String owned, &str params. No Cow.
instructions.notes: String handling rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Per-module Result with custom error enums (Display + Error). assert! only for logic-error invariants.
instructions.notes: Error handling rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Import order: std then external then crate::. Explicit names. use super::* only in #[cfg(test)].
instructions.notes: Import rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Construction: new() primary, builder with_*(self) -> Self for optional config.
instructions.notes: Construction convention from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: One primary type per file. Private mod + public use re-exports in mod.rs.
instructions.notes: Module organization from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: pub(crate) default visibility. pub only for library API consumed by sosumi-7.
instructions.notes: Visibility rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Use expect('reason') unless invariant is obvious from context. Bare unwrap() fine in tests and same-line proofs.
instructions.notes: Unwrap rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Fix warnings (remove dead code, prefix _, apply clippy fix). Suppress only genuine false positives with a comment.
instructions.notes: Warning handling rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md (Pixel fidelity): Reproduce C++ integer formulas exactly in blend/coverage/interpolation/sampling. Use (x*257+0x8073)>>16 not f64 division. Wrap in newtypes. Named constants with derivations. No f64 approximations in compositing pipeline.
instructions.notes: Detailed pixel fidelity rule from CLAUDE.md. Reinforces i-18.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md (Geometry fidelity): Same algorithm and operation order on golden-tested paths. Iterator::sum OK. Clamp/min/max must preserve C++ boundary values.
instructions.notes: Geometry fidelity rule from CLAUDE.md. Matches contract GEOMETRY layer.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md (State fidelity): Fully idiomatic Rust for state logic, data structures, ownership, API surface. Preserve behavioral contracts (return values, side effects, ordering). Adapt syntax freely.
instructions.notes: State fidelity rule from CLAUDE.md. Reinforces i-19.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: When in doubt, check if the function's output feeds a golden test. If yes, port the C++ formula exactly. If no, write idiomatic Rust.
instructions.notes: Tiebreaker rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use #[allow(...)] or #[expect(...)]. Fix the warning instead. UNLESS warning is for too many arguments (which is allowed).
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use Arc/Mutex -- single-threaded UI tree.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md. Overlaps with i-66.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use Cow -- use String/&str.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md. Overlaps with i-67.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use glob imports (use foo::*) except use super::* in tests.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT truncate color math to u8 mid-calculation.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md. Critical for pixel fidelity.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use f64 in blend/coverage/interpolation paths -- use C++ integer arithmetic.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md. Reinforces i-75.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use Rayon/parallel iteration on golden-tested code paths.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use assert! for recoverable errors.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md. Overlaps with i-68.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Do NOT use --no-verify on commits.
instructions.notes: Do NOT rule from CLAUDE.md. Pre-commit hook must run.

instructions.text: CLAUDE.md: Pre-commit hook runs cargo fmt (auto-applied) then clippy -D warnings then cargo-nextest ntr. Do not skip with --no-verify. If a commit fails, fix the cause and retry.
instructions.notes: Pre-commit hook rule from CLAUDE.md.

instructions.text: Log format: feature entry must include feature_id, description, MATCHes count, MISMATCHes count, MISSINGs count, fixes applied list, and tests added count.
instructions.notes: Specific fields required in the run log entry.

instructions.text: Log entry must be appended (not overwritten) to .workflow/widget-comparison/run-log.md.
instructions.notes: Append semantics for the log.

instructions.text: Commit message format: 'audit: <feature_id> <summary>'.
instructions.notes: Commit message convention.

instructions.text: Include prompts/master-contract.json in the git add for each commit.
instructions.notes: The contract JSON tracks progress and must be committed each time.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step: For MISMATCH fixes, apply minimal changes to the Rust code (not rewrite).
instructions.notes: The word 'minimally' in the step constrains fix scope.

instructions.text: Phase 2 step: For NEEDS_IMPLEMENTATION items, include a scope estimate.
instructions.notes: Scope estimates help prioritize implementation.

instructions.text: Phase 4 anti-pattern check: verify modifier keys are set correctly for modifier-dependent tests.
instructions.notes: Specific anti-pattern from the 5-point checklist.

instructions.text: Phase 4 anti-pattern check: verify test exercises actual behavior, not just compilation.
instructions.notes: Specific anti-pattern from the 5-point checklist.

instructions.text: Phase 4 anti-pattern check: verify test would FAIL if implementation were subtly wrong.
instructions.notes: Mutation-testing-like criterion from the 5-point checklist.

instructions.text: Phase 1 step: The report for each method must use one of four categories: MATCH, MISMATCH, SUSPECT, or MISSING.
instructions.notes: SUSPECT is a category from the contract steps that is not mentioned in the prompt .md (which only mentions MATCH/MISMATCH/MISSING).
